name: Prevent Large File Reads
description: Warns before reading files larger than 100KB into context. Suggests using head/grep/tail instead of cat on large files.

then.command: CMD=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); FILE=$(echo "$CMD" | grep -oE '(cat|less|more)\s+([^ |>]+)' | awk '{print $NF}'); [ -z "$FILE" ] || [ ! -f "$FILE" ] && exit 0; SIZE=$(du -k "$FILE" 2>/dev/null | cut -f1); [ "${SIZE:-0}" -gt 100 ] && echo "WARNING: $FILE is ${SIZE}KB. Use head -50, grep, or tail instead to avoid context bloat." >&2; exit 0
